title: Ordered-CatBoost raw-category specialist
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality entities (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, compound interactions).
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality entities (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, compound interactions). CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are designed to exploit such features while reducing target-leakage bias from naive encodings.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier on mostly raw columns plus a few simple interactions (`Race_Year`, `Driver_Compound`, `Race_Compound`, `Stint`, peer-relative numerics). Avoid manual target encoding; let ordered CTRs and category combinations do the work. Compare plain logloss vs AUC-optimized selection, then ensemble OOF scores with the incumbent model.
expected_effect: Potentially stronger generalization on sparse drivers/races and a meaningfully different error pattern for blending.
risk: May underperform if CV grouping is weak or if too many handcrafted features drown out CatBoost’s categorical advantages; runtime and tuning can be touchy.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
